summary: Excellent front‑of‑house capture of a inspired May ’93 Bay Area show with a barn‑burning Jack Straw, soaring Loser solo and killer Saint>Terrapin sequence.
review: The recording (a front‑of‑house mix) faithfully captures one of the high‑water‑mark shows from May 1993, highlighted by an electrifying first set and a textbook second set. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s loud, searing guitar work, Vince’s sweet‑spot keyboards and Phil’s monster bass, while noting standout performances of Jack Straw, Loser, Music Never Stopped and the Saint>Terrapin suite. Technical quality is praised as clear and well‑balanced.

ai_rating.rationale: The single detailed review is overwhelmingly positive about both performance and sound, indicating a high‑quality show captured with an excellent front‑of‑house recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house (FOB) mix described as capturing the performance perfectly; clear, balanced sound with minimal MIDI artifacts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set called "inspired and rocking" with strong openings; second set described as a textbook spring‑'93 set, stellar from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Loud, searing guitar tone; deep solo on Loser; one of the best late‑show Aiko performances.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as a monster; delivers a righteous Tom Thumb Blues and strong overall bass work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince finds his sweet spot; his playing is highlighted throughout, especially in the AON jam and Music Never Stopped.
